summary.intent: Build a desktop UI for the Gossamer checkpoint/session monitoring tool, starting with routing and a new timeline screen, then migrating the entire app from React Native/Expo to Electron for better component library flexibility, and iteratively improving the sessions view to use V2 database tables.
summary.outcome: Successfully migrated from Expo to Electron+Vite+React Router, implemented file-based routing equivalent, added CheckpointTimeline screen, fixed WebSocket live-update bug (server only broadcast on Event table changes, not CheckpointMetadata), and refactored sessions view to use CheckpointSessionMetadata with branch and intent fields from V2 tables.

repo: expo-router defaults to an 'app/' directory for routes, which collides with the project root being named 'app/' — workaround is setting 'root' in the expo-router plugin config
The WebSocket broadcast poller in src/server.ts only watched the Event table, not CheckpointMetadata — so checkpoint updates never triggered live UI refreshes
V2 checkpoint tables: CheckpointMetadata, CheckpointSessionMetadata (has branch, sessionId), CheckpointSessionSummary (has intent) — these are separate from the old checkpointSession table
The server's V2_SESSION_INCLUDE already includes the summary relation; just needed to thread it through mapSession and the response type
runIndex() was only called after 30s delay on startup — causing empty UI on fresh server start
The /tmp/gossamer-checkpoints worktree gets cleared between macOS restarts, causing a non-fatal git worktree warning on server start
Session model does NOT have a branch field — branch is only on CheckpointSessionMetadata, joined by sessionId

code.finding: WebSocket broadcast poller — was only tracking max Event.id, not CheckpointMetadata.id, causing silent checkpoint updates
Session type needed branch (from CheckpointSessionMetadata) and intent (from CheckpointSessionSummary) fields added
RN shim layer: View→div(flex-col), Text→div, TouchableOpacity→div[role=button], StyleSheet.create is a passthrough — allows near-mechanical port of RN screen code

workflow: electron-vite outputs to out/ by default (out/main/index.js), not dist-electron/ — package.json main field must match
Background task exit code 144 = process killed by pkill during restart cycle — not an error, just a stale notification
In Electron dev mode, ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL env var is set by electron-vite and should be used to loadURL instead of loadFile
MemoryRouter is appropriate for Electron (no real URL bar); pass title via navigate(path, { state: { title } }) and read it in Layout via useLocation().state
MarkdownView must use semantic HTML block elements (h1, ul, pre) not nested divs/spans to avoid block-inside-inline rendering issues

friction: expo-router naming collision with existing app/ project directory required discovering and using the underdocumented 'root' plugin option
expo toolchain automatically stripped tsconfig.json include array additions
package.json main field pointed to wrong electron-vite output path (dist-electron vs out/main)
Multiple stale background task failure notifications throughout the session created noise (exit code 144 = killed by pkill, not real failures)
Context window was exhausted mid-session, requiring a summary handoff that interrupted the live-update debugging flow
open_items: branch field on Session model: currently fetched via join on CheckpointSessionMetadata but not stored on Session — if needed at query time without join, requires schema migration
Git worktree /tmp/gossamer-checkpoints gets cleared on macOS restart — server has a prune fix written but only applied on next rebuild
Checkpoint indexer only runs if server started with --repo-dir flag — should document this requirement or make it auto-detect
No Mantine or other web component library has been added yet — the Electron migration was motivated by this but not yet executed
SessionDetail screen may still reference old navigation patterns that weren't verified after the Electron migration
CheckpointTimeline color-per-branch hash function is a simple implementation — may want to use a proper color palette
